articles.title: Signal in the age of infinite noise
articles.description: The anonymous person behind the popular X account, SightBringer, explains his take on the most valuable and least understood asset of the market.

articles.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy buys 3,273 bitcoin as it inches closer to its 1 million target
articles.description: Strategy, the largest corporate holder of bitcoin in the world, now holds 818,334 BTC, which it said it purchased for nearly $62 billion at an average price of roughly $75,537 per token.

articles.title: Bitmine buys $236 million in ether as Tom Lee touts ETH as 'wartime store of value'
articles.description: The firm has bought more than 5 million in ETH in just 10 months while most digital asset treasury companies have stopped accumulating.

articles.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
articles.description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

articles.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Stellar (XLM) drops 3.4%, leading index lower
articles.description: NEAR Protocol (NEAR), down 2.9%, was also among the underperformers.

articles.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
articles.description: The phone could go in mass production in 2028, an analyst says.

articles.title: First 21-week trend line reclaim since October 2025: Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
articles.description: Bitcoin price action sealed its first weekly candle close above a 21-week moving average trend line since it traded near $115,000 in October 2025.

articles.title: Pudgy Penguins rally coincides with token unlock as analyst flags exit liquidity risk
articles.description: DNTV Research's Bradley Park says bullish ecosystem news gave large holders the liquidity they needed to sell into a recent PENGU unlock.

articles.title: Bitcoin funds take in $933 million as crypto ETFs hit highest AUM since February
articles.description: Total assets under management across crypto funds rose to $155 billion, the highest level since February 1, though still well below the $263 billion peak from October 2025.

articles.title: ETH price up 10% in April, so why is Ethereum Foundation selling?
articles.description: ETH's price may drop 15% or more in the coming days as it paints a convincing bearish reversal pattern on its daily chart.

articles.title: Kbank teams with Ripple on overseas blockchain remittance trial
articles.description: Kbank partnered with Ripple to test blockchain-based overseas remittances as South Korean companies prepare for new stablecoin and digital asset rules.

articles.title: From SWIFT to onchain: South Korea’s biggest digital bank taps Ripple for high-speed global transfers
articles.description: KBank is using Palisade, Ripple's software-as-a-service wallet that was acquired earlier this year as part of Ripple's $4 billion in crypto-related investments.

articles.title: MiCA-licensed Banking Circle joins bank stablecoin settlement race in Europe
articles.description: Banking Circle's stablecoin settlement launch follows its CASP approval, entering a crowded market with SocGen, Sygnum and a 12-bank euro stablecoin consortium.

articles.title: Bitcoin hits wall at $80,000, one analyst says the pullback is temporary

articles.title: Bitcoin leads $1.2B weekly inflows into crypto investment products
articles.description: Crypto ETPs see $1.2 billion inflows in fourth straight week as Bitcoin leads gains and blockchain equity ETFs hit record demand, CoinShares reported.

articles.title: EU’s largest measures against Russia yet include escalation of crypto sanctions evasion
articles.description: The European Union noted that Russia has become increasingly reliant on cryptocurrency to circumvent sanctions.

articles.title: Bitcoin reverses from $79,500 as oil surge triggers broader crypto selloff
articles.description: BTC fails at $80,000 and drops 2% as rising oil prices weigh on sentiment with altcoins leading losses across a volatile session.
